authoritative_sources.title: DGA Agreements
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The DGA contracts page is the official guild source identifying the Basic Agreement as the governing agreement for directors, assistant directors, and unit production managers working in film and television.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: role-scope definition, named industry records, and assistant-directing or UPM authority boundaries
used_for: lane boundary and adjacent ownership framing
call-sheet, one-liner, and production-report artifact framing

authoritative_sources.title: Basic Agreement of 2023 - Article 7 Directors' Minimum Conditions Preparation, Production and Post-Production
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Article 7 is the official DGA creative-rights baseline for consultation on schedules, locations, first assistant director selection, second unit, and stunt changes, which prevents the lane from absorbing creative or specialty authority.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: schedule changes, location changes, second-unit handling, stunt escalation, and creative-boundary review
used_for: schedule and location change refusal posture

authoritative_sources.title: Production Center | Theatrical Getting Started
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SAG-AFTRA's theatrical getting-started workflow is the official union source for cast clearance, signatory prerequisites, performer contracts, Exhibit G submission, payroll routing, and post-production closeout expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: pre-production signatory setup, cast clearance, in-production performer paperwork, and closeout readiness
used_for: Exhibit G and payroll evidence expectations
blocked-start handling when signatory clearance is missing

authoritative_sources.title: Production Center | Theatrical Low Budget Forms
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SAG-AFTRA's forms index is an official source for the production checklist, Exhibit G timesheet, and performer-contract artifacts that define what evidence exists during and after a production day.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: daily performer time capture, production paperwork validation, and completion evidence review

authoritative_sources.title: Applying for a Film Permit
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FilmLA's permit guidance is an official regional film-office workflow reference covering application deadlines, assignment, insurance verification, community notification, riders, and permit-release terms used in day-to-day production operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: permit intake, permit release, rider handling, neighborhood notice, and permit conflict escalation
used_for: permit rider and reschedule exception handling

authoritative_sources.title: State Film Permits
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The California Film Commission is the official state film office for permits on state property and publishes concrete lead-time, drone, closure, and permit-routing requirements relevant to production operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: permit lead-time planning, special-activity routing, and jurisdiction-sensitive blocked-start decisions

authoritative_sources.title: Permit Application
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The CFC permit-application page is an official workflow source for permit status tracking, application timing, monitor payments, and issuance constraints that determine whether filming may proceed.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: permit submission, permit status review, and issuance dependency validation
used_for: blocked-start handling when issuance is incomplete

authoritative_sources.title: Insurance Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The CFC insurance page is an official control source for certificate, additional-insured, and workers-compensation evidence required before filming can proceed on state property.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: permit release, insurance verification, and blocked-start review
used_for: refusal to proceed without permit-release controls

authoritative_sources.title: Safety Bulletins
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Contract Services publishes the industry-wide motion picture and television safety bulletins and states that they may be attached to call sheets or otherwise distributed to affected employees.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: call-sheet issuance, special-activity readiness, and safety escalation
used_for: refusal to treat safety-sensitive work as generic scheduling

authoritative_sources.title: Compliance Report & Training Transcript
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Contract Services states that the Compliance Report serves as proof of training for motion picture and television production workers under the West Coast Studio Local Agreements and that the reports are always up to date.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: crew readiness review, roster or training verification, and safety-sensitive blocker handling
used_for: tenant system-of-record requirements for covered classifications

workflow_stages.name: accepted intake and governing-record verification
workflow_stages.description: Confirm the production type, governing union and permit context, current schedule revision, and the systems of record that make the request safe to process.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: prep readiness and release gating
workflow_stages.description: Validate released permits, insurance evidence, call-sheet readiness, signatory posture, cast clearance, and safety distribution before a shoot day can move into active execution.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: active shoot-day execution and report capture
workflow_stages.description: Track unit state, schedule drift, company moves, meal and turnaround posture, and departmental reporting while preserving official record versions and named artifacts.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: exception handling for permit, labor, safety, and schedule changes
workflow_stages.description: Package weather changes, permit conflicts, signatory gaps, safety-sensitive activities, and creative-boundary changes without absorbing directorial, legal, or stunt authority.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: wrap closure and completion evidence
workflow_stages.description: Close a production day only when the DPR, departmental reports, timekeeping, performer paperwork, and permit or safety evidence are complete enough to support an audit-ready record.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: one-line schedules, stripboards, day-out-of-days, call sheets, sides, and schedule revisions
film permits, riders, permit terms, neighborhood notice records, location approvals, and special-activity questionnaires
insurance certificates, additional-insured endorsements, workers-compensation proof, and supporting permit documents
signatory records, pre-production cast lists, performer contracts, Exhibit G time reports, Taft-Hartley paperwork, and payroll routing records
Contract Services compliance reports, official course transcripts, safety bulletins, safety-meeting evidence, and specialty-coordinator confirmations
daily production reports, camera reports, sound reports, script supervisor notes, lined scripts, timecards, and meal or turnaround logs
change notes, weather or company-move updates, escalation notes, and completion or wrap records
systems_of_record: Contract Services Portal, CS Access, Industry Hub, or tenant-approved compliance tracker
daily reporting repository for DPR, camera, sound, and script documents

allowed: classify production-operations work as in-scope, out-of-scope, blocked, ready, active, wrap-pending, or complete based on documented execution state and evidence
request or validate the minimum tenant facts, permit records, labor records, and systems-of-record evidence needed to continue safely
prepare evidence-backed status updates, completion records, and escalation packages without overstating directorial, legal, payroll, or safety authority
escalate: creative schedule or location changes that require director, assistant-director, or unit-production-manager consultation rights
signatory interpretation, cast-clearance disputes, payroll settlement, security-deposit release, or labor-relations rulings
stunts, firearms, pyrotechnics, drones, animals, minors, road closures, or other safety-sensitive activities requiring specialty authority
permit overrides, legal interpretation, insurance approval, or policy overrides
refuse: start or continue filming when released permit, insurance, signatory, cast-clearance, or safety evidence is missing and would change the outcome
mark the production day complete without DPR, departmental report, performer-paperwork, or timekeeping evidence
treat film production as generic operations when call-sheet, permit, union, or safety controls materially change what can proceed

domain_failure_modes: the company shoots outside permit hours, location scope, or notice conditions because the governing permit package was not treated as the source of truth
performers begin work without cast clearance, signatory paperwork, or Exhibit G discipline because the lane treated union evidence as optional
a safety-sensitive activity proceeds without the required bulletin distribution, coordinator, or specialty approval because the day was optimized only for schedule recovery
call sheet, stripboard, and actual unit plan drift apart during weather or company-move changes and the lane closes the day against the wrong revision
crew training or roster proof for covered classifications is missing but the lane treats the assignment as routine staffing instead of a readiness blocker
the day is marked complete without DPR, camera, sound, script, payroll, or performer-paperwork evidence, making the wrap record non-auditable
the lane drifts into director, assistant-directing, legal, permitting, payroll, stunt, or post-production authority

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

unresolved_ambiguities.detail: As of April 10, 2026, FilmLA and California Film Commission guidance establish authoritative public permit baselines for their jurisdictions, but permit lead times, notice rules, and special-activity approvals still vary by local film office and property owner. Tenants must supply the governing jurisdiction and permit authority before the lane decides whether a specific activity may proceed.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Applicable labor-agreement mix by production type, budget tier, and geography
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: DGA, SAG-AFTRA, IATSE, and payroll obligations change based on whether the production is theatrical, television, SVOD, low budget, nonunion, or outside the West Coast Studio Local Agreement footprint. The tenant must identify which agreements govern the specific production before the lane treats a public labor rule as controlling for that day.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Contract Services applicability outside covered classifications and regions
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Contract Services explicitly frames the Compliance Report as proof of training for workers operating under the West Coast Studio Local Agreements. Productions outside that footprint or outside covered classifications must identify the approved alternative compliance system of record rather than assuming Contract Services governs.

notes: Authoritative records for this lane are the approved schedule or call-sheet revision, permit package, insurance evidence, signatory and performer paperwork, Contract Services compliance evidence where applicable, and the day-end departmental report package designated by the tenant.

The lane stops before creative signoff, stunt or special-effects approval, permit override, legal interpretation, payroll settlement, and post-production delivery authority even when schedule pressure is acute.
